239.reason: [Missing error handling when char constant is malformed; also fails to advance index properly in all cases, and doesn't handle single quote followed by EOF or newline]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing error handling when char constant is malformed; also fails to advance index properly in all cases, and doesn't handle single quote followed by EOF or newline]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [If the character constant is malformed (e.g., only one quote or more than one character between quotes), the lexer fails to detect the error and does not advance the index properly, potentially causing infinite loops or missing error reporting. Additionally, it doesn't handle cases like '\'' correctly when input length is insufficient, and doesn't report error type 'a' or any relevant lexical error for invalid character literals.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This code does not handle invalid or unterminated character constants. If the next character after '\'' is not followed by a closing '\'', no error is reported, and the lexer silently skips it, which violates the requirement to report lexical errors. Additionally, it fails to advance `index` properly in the malformed case, potentially causing infinite loops or missed errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing error handling when char constant is malformed (e.g., single quote alone or incomplete), and missing increment of index in all cases leading to infinite loop or skipped characters]

238.reason: [If the character constant is malformed (e.g., only one quote or more than one character between quotes), no error is reported and the lexer may skip handling it entirely, violating the requirement to detect lexical errors. Additionally, if input length is insufficient (e.g., file ends after the opening quote), this code does not handle it and may cause incorrect behavior or miss an error.]
